oracle_assumptions.description: ERC721.ownerOf() calls are authoritative and return correct ownership
oracle_assumptions.risk: If NFT contract returns incorrect ownership, directors can be impersonated
oracle_assumptions.mitigation: Try-catch blocks around ownerOf calls, fallback to address(0) for invalid tokens

oracle_assumptions.risk: Non-standard ERC20 could break accounting or enable reentrancy

admin_assumptions.description: Chamber controls its own ProxyAdmin after deployment
admin_assumptions.risk: If ProxyAdmin ownership not transferred, upgrades can be blocked or hijacked
admin_assumptions.mitigation: Registry contract transfers ownership in acceptAdmin()

admin_assumptions.description: Initial seat configuration is reasonable (not 0 or excessive)
admin_assumptions.risk: Zero seats = no governance, excessive seats = governance dilution
admin_assumptions.mitigation: Bounds checking in initialize() and updateSeats()

trust_boundary_assumptions.description: NFT holders are distinct entities, not controlled by single adversary
trust_boundary_assumptions.risk: Sybil attack where one entity controls majority of NFTs
trust_boundary_assumptions.mitigation: No protocol-level mitigation, relies on NFT distribution

trust_boundary_assumptions.description: Directors won't collude to drain funds

trust_boundary_assumptions.risk: Smart contract directors could have signature validation bugs
trust_boundary_assumptions.mitigation: DirectorAuth hash validation, try-catch around isValidSignature
